'''Mitchell Ronald Edwin Brown''' (born 7 November 1987) is a former Australian professional footballer who played for the [[Warrington Wolves]] in the [[Super League]]. He previously played for the [[Canterbury-Bankstown Bulldogs]], the [[Cronulla-Sutherland Sharks]], the [[Leigh Centurions]] and the [[Wests Tigers]].

==Background==
Brown was born in [[Sutherland, New South Wales]], Australia.

==Playing career==
Brown began playing junior rugby league for his local club side the [[Gymea Gorillas]]. He went to school at [[Endeavour Sports High School]] in the [[Sutherland Shire]], working his way up through the age groups and representing the school at fullback in their back to back [[Arrive Alive Cup]] tournament wins.{{citation needed|date=June 2017}}

While attending Endeavour, Brown played for the [[Australia national schoolboys rugby league team|Australian Schoolboys team]] in 2005.<ref>{{cite web|title=SportingPulse Homepage for Australian Secondary Schools Rugby League|url=http://www.sportingpulse.com/assoc_page.cgi?c=7-2130-0-0-0&sID=26424|accessdate=2008-10-10| publisher=SportingPulse}}</ref>

===Cronulla-Sutherland Sharks===
At the beginning of the [[2006 NRL season]], many pundits earmarked Brown as playing a major part in the Sharks new season campaign.{{citation needed|date=June 2017}} However, for the majority of the season, Brown had to settle for playing in the lower grades for the Cronulla side after being behind [[David Simmons (rugby league)|David Simmons]] in the order for the {{rlp|fb}} spot and both [[Darren Albert]] and [[Luke Covell]] on the wings. His chance didn't come until late in the season with the dropping of [[Darren Albert]] allowing Brown to make his debut on the wing for the Sharks.{{citation needed|date=June 2017}}

2007 was a successful season for Brown, as he played in 14 of the 24 first games for the Sharks. An injury to fullback [[Brett Kearney]] early in the season gave Brown the opportunity at his preferred position of fullback. In 2008, Brown had limited opportunities as Brett Kearney played the majority of the season.{{citation needed|date=June 2017}}

During the 2009 season, Brown played the majority of his games at fullback, due to Brett Kearney's season-ending injury towards the start of the season. On 17 July, Brown signed with the Wests Tigers for 2010 and 2011.<ref>{{cite news|publisher=ABC Grandstand|title=Tigers poach Sharks' Brown|url=http://www.abc.net.au/news/stories/2009/07/17/2629492.htm?site=sport&section=all|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20121109211831/http://www.abc.net.au/news/stories/2009/07/17/2629492.htm?site=sport&section=all|url-status=dead|archive-date=9 November 2012|accessdate=2009-07-18|date=17 July 2009}}</ref>

===Wests Tigers===
Brown was left out of the Wests Tigers side for the first games of the [[2010 NRL season]], but injuries to players like [[Tim Moltzen]]<ref>{{cite news|newspaper=Herald Sun|title=Mitch Brown: I'm not scared of you, Jarryd Hayne|author=Christian Nicolussi|url=http://www.heraldsun.com.au/sport/nrl/mitch-brown-im-not-scared-of-you-jarryd-hayne/story-e6frfgbo-1225844483530|accessdate=30 September 2010|date=24 March 2010}}</ref> saw him make 20 appearances throughout season, and he was chosen as fullback for all three of the club's final matches. Noted for his versatility,<ref>{{cite news|newspaper=The Sydney Morning Herald|title=Brown may be used as bait in Moltzen battle|author=Michael Chammas|url=http://www.smh.com.au/rugby-league/st-george-illawarra-dragons/brown-may-be-used-as-bait-in-moltzen-battle-20111018-1lyso.html|accessdate=1 January 2012|date=19 October 2011}}</ref> Brown made 20 appearances in 2011, playing wing and centre when the Wests Tigers suffered long injuries to [[Chris Lawrence (rugby league)|Chris Lawrence]] and [[Lote Tuqiri (rugby, born 1979)|Lote Tuqiri]]. Having started the 2012 season with the Wests Tigers, Brown was released to join [[Canterbury Bulldogs]] after round one. Tigers CEO Stephen Humphreys said, "he felt he needed to position himself somewhere where he could secure a starting spot. His preferred position is centre."<ref>{{cite news|author=Alicia Sylvester|work=Campbelltown Macarthur Advertiser|title=Mitch Brown leaves Wests Tigers|url=http://www.macarthuradvertiser.com.au/news/local/news/general/mitch-brown-leaves-wests-tigers/2477195.aspx|accessdate=10 June 2012|date=5 March 2012}}</ref>

===Canterbury-Bankstown Bulldogs===
An [[anterior cruciate ligament]] (ACL) knee injury early in the 2012 season saw him play no games that year.<ref>{{cite web|work=SEN|title=Preview: Bulldogs v Titans|url=http://www.sen.com.au/display-article/Preview-Bulldogs-v-Titans/43331|accessdate=10 June 2012|date=10 May 2012|url-status=dead|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20131202223559/http://www.sen.com.au/display-article/Preview-Bulldogs-v-Titans/43331|archivedate=2 December 2013}}</ref>

Playing all but one game on the wing in 2013, Brown scored 12 tries from 24 appearances, both personal bests. In round 7, playing against old club Cronulla, Brown scored his first ever double. He went on the score another two doubles before the end of the year.{{citation needed|date=June 2017}}

Brown was on the wing again in 2014, apart from a 4-week stint at fullback when [[Sam Perrett]] was unavailable. He played in all four of the Bulldogs post-season games, including the [[2014 NRL Grand Final|grand final]] which they lost 30–6 against South Sydney.

===Return to Cronulla===
Brown returned to Cronulla for the 2015 season, saying, "I've lived here my whole life, I've never moved, I just travelled. I've been a Sharks supporter since I can remember and I was born in Sutherland Hospital so I'm a Shire boy through and through. I've always supported the Sharks and I always wanted to come back when I left. I'm the only local junior apart from Anthony Moratis."<ref>{{cite web| work= nrl.com| title=Mitch Brown loves junior rugby league| author=Joe Riordan| url=http://www.nrl.com/mitch-brown-loves-junior-rugby-league/tabid/10874/newsid/84617/default.aspx|accessdate=19 October 2015|date=19 March 2015}}</ref>

After being chosen on the wing for the first game of the season, Brown made just one further appearance for the 2015 season after being dropped due to poor form.<ref>{{cite news| work= The Sydney Morning Herald| title=The NRL's forgotten 13 - the players you may not even know are at your club| author=Michael Carayannis| url=http://www.smh.com.au/rugby-league/league-news/the-nrls-forgotten-13--the-players-you-may-not-even-know-are-at-your-club-20151015-gkak1o.html|accessdate=19 October 2015|date=17 October 2015}}</ref>

He joined the [[Leigh Centurions]] part way through the 2016 season. In November 2017 he signed a one-year deal with [[Warrington Wolves]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://warringtonwolves.com/club-news/wire-sign-winger-brown/|title=Wire sign winger Brown|publisher=Warrington Wolves|date=16 November 2017|accessdate=7 December 2017}}</ref>
